# ManteCurated Live

Real-time Hive blockchain curation analytics dashboard. Built with Next.js 14 (App Router), TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and @hiveio/dhive.

## What it does

Shows any Hive account's curation activity: voting power, vote value, account stats, recent votes, top authors voted, and a **Curation Quality Score (CQS)** — a 1–100 metric that measures how good a curator an account is.

## Stack

- Next.js 14 App Router, React 18, TypeScript
- @hiveio/dhive for Hive RPC calls (multi-node failover)
- Tailwind CSS + Recharts for UI
- No database — all data comes from the Hive blockchain in real time

## Project layout

```
app/
  page.tsx              # Landing page
  [username]/page.tsx   # Dashboard for any Hive account (e.g. /@mantequilla)
  s/single/page.tsx     # Single curator view
  c/compare/page.tsx    # Multi-curator comparison
  api/
    cqs/route.ts        # REST API: GET /api/cqs?account=<username>

components/
  Dashboard.tsx             # Main client component, orchestrates all data fetching
  CurationQualityScoreCard.tsx
  VotingPowerGauge.tsx
  AccountStatsCard.tsx
  RecentVotesFeed.tsx
  TopAuthorsChart.tsx
  CuratorComparison.tsx

lib/
  hive.ts           # All Hive RPC calls (getAccount, getVoteHistory, etc.)
  curation-score.ts # CQS algorithm
  votemath.ts       # VP%, vote value, HP, RC calculations
  format.ts         # Number formatting helpers

types/
  hive.ts           # All TypeScript interfaces
```

## CQS algorithm (lib/curation-score.ts)

Scores a curator 1–100 using a geometric mean of three sub-scores, each 0–1:

- **Breadth (B)** = min(uniqueAuthors / 100, 1.0) — diversity of authors voted
- **Distribution (D)** = 1 − Gini(authorWeights) — how evenly vote weight is spread
- **Anti-Self (S)** = 1 − (selfVoteWeight / totalVoteWeight) — penalises self-voting

```
rawScore = (B × D × S)^(1/3)
CQS      = ceil(rawScore × 99) + 1     # range: 1–100
```

Geometric mean means you cannot compensate a weak dimension with a strong one. Only upvotes (weight > 0) in the last 7 days are counted. Returns `CurationQualityScore` (see types/hive.ts).

## REST API

### GET /api/cqs?account=\<username\>

Returns the CQS for any Hive account. Responses are cached in-memory for 15 minutes per account.

**Request**
```
GET /api/cqs?account=mantequilla
```
Leading `@` is stripped automatically. Account must match `[a-z0-9._-]{3,16}`.

**Success response (200)**
```json
{
  "account": "mantequilla",
  "score": 73,
  "rawScore": 0.741,
  "subScores": {
    "breadth": 0.85,
    "distribution": 0.79,
    "antiSelf": 1.0
  },
  "metrics": {
    "totalVoteWeight": 184200,
    "uniqueAuthors": 85,
    "top50Weight": 184200,
    "selfVoteWeight": 0,
    "voteCount": 142,
    "giniCoefficient": 0.21,
    "timeWindow": {
      "startDate": "2026-06-05T12:00:00.000Z",
      "endDate": "2026-06-12T12:00:00.000Z",
      "daysIncluded": 7
    }
  },
  "topAuthors": [
    { "author": "someone", "voteCount": 5, "totalWeight": 5000 }
  ],
  "cachedAt": "2026-06-12T18:00:00.000Z",
  "fromCache": false,
  "cacheExpiresIn": 900
}
```

**Error responses**
- `400` — missing or invalid account name
- `404` — account has no calculable score
- `502` — Hive RPC nodes unreachable

`fromCache: true` means the result came from the in-memory cache. `cacheExpiresIn` is seconds until the cache entry expires.

## Hive RPC (lib/hive.ts)

Uses a dhive `Client` with four failover nodes:
```
https://api.hive.blog
https://api.deathwing.me
https://hive-api.arcange.eu
https://api.openhive.network
```

Key functions:
- `getAccount(username)` — full account object
- `getVoteHistory(username, days=7)` — batches up to 20×1000 history ops, returns vote ops cast BY the user sorted newest-first
- `getGlobalProperties()` — needed for HP conversions
- `getRewardFund()` — needed for vote value
- `getHivePrice()` — CoinGecko USD price, fallback $0.30

## URL routing

- `/@username` and `/username` both work (rewrite in next.config.js)
- Curator comparison: `/c/compare`
- Single view: `/s/single`

## Running locally

```bash
npm install
npm run dev   # http://localhost:3000
```

No environment variables required — all data is public on-chain.
